name: Action Against Hunger USA
mission: Our mission is to save, improve and protect lives by eliminating hunger through the prevention, detection and treatment of undernutrition, especially during and after emergency crises caused by situations of conflict, displacement, poverty, discrimination, inequality, or natural disaster.
theoryOfChange: Action Against Hunger USA has a published 'International Strategic Plan (2021-2025)' which serves as a strategic framework. This document provides a shared direction and framework for their work over five years, outlining how they will achieve their vision of a world without hunger. It details their goals and the targets they aim to achieve, focusing on long-term change through mitigating key drivers of hunger, including climate change, gender inequality, conflict, and poverty. The plan describes a multisectoral approach to address the immediate, underlying, and root causes of undernutrition and its effects. Action Against Hunger USA, as part of the global network, explicitly refers to and links this International Strategic Plan on its website.

scoreSummary: Action Against Hunger USA shows exceptional impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 81% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 34/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, humanitarian cause area

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 81% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 81% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: Action Against Hunger USA shows exceptional impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $160,756,238 and operates programs in nutrition, health, and food security.
Action Against Hunger USA focuses on treating undernutrition and monitoring global acute malnutrition rates.
The organization addresses severe acute food insecurity in high-urgency regions like South Sudan.
The organization accepts general charitable donations and Sadaqah.
The charity maintains a 95.0/100 score, a program expense ratio of 89.7%, 10.2 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a strong overall rating due to its high impact score of 49/50 and its exceptional financial health, characterized by low overhead and high program spending. It is recognized as a Sadaqah-eligible entity for donors seeking to support humanitarian relief.
areas_for_improvement: Increasing alignment with specific donor preferences to maximize the 34/50 alignment score
Expanding public reporting on long-term outcome data beyond immediate service delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 34/50, the organization shows a strong track record in high-urgency humanitarian causes [1][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, maintaining a low cost per beneficiary and a program expense ratio of 89.7% [1][9]. Its financial health is robust, supported by 10.2 months of working capital and a high Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100 [9].

baseline_narrative.headline: Action Against Hunger USA is a highly efficient humanitarian organization dedicated to eliminating life-threatening undernutrition through direct nutritional and health interventions.
strengths: Exceptional financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 89.7%
High fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.05 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 10.2 months of working capital
High Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100 indicating strong accountability
baseline_narrative.summary: Action Against Hunger USA operates as a global humanitarian organization focused on preventing, detecting, and treating undernutrition, particularly in crisis-affected regions [1][2]. The organization manages a diverse portfolio of programs including Nutrition and Health, Water, Sanitation & Hygiene, and Food Security & Livelihoods to address the root causes of hunger [1][9].

A significant portion of expenses, approximately <cite id="19">81% of total burn</cite>, is recorded as domestic despite the international nature of the mission.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains a strong 47-year track record and high efficiency scores to weather funding fluctuations.
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="8">recent 20% drop in revenue</cite> and the high <cite id="19">domestic expense ratio</cite> relative to international grants.

confidence.notes: Strong alignment between financial filings and third-party ratings.

known_gaps: FY2024 expense breakdown not yet fully audited

alignment.improvement: To better serve specific donor segments, the charity could <cite id="20">target more remote, underserved populations</cite>.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="13">20/20 BBB standard compliance</cite>, <cite id="16">fully independent board</cite>, and <cite id="14">Platinum Candid status</cite>.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly direct, with <cite id="15">$5.46 spent per beneficiary</cite> and a <cite id="4">89.7% program ratio</cite> that maximizes field-level aid.
impact.improvement: The charity could improve by <cite id="20">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their sadaqah to support large-scale, scientifically-proven nutritional interventions.
donor_motivations: Want to support evidence-based malnutrition treatment
Prefer established organizations with 40+ years of experience
Focus on high-impact humanitarian relief in crisis zones
giving_considerations: Large scale means gifts are pooled for global response
Recent revenue declines may affect new program expansion
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors specifically looking to fulfill zakat obligations or those seeking small, grassroots community projects.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A reflects <cite id="24">10 tracked outcome metrics</cite> and verified field data like <cite id="10">Somalia malnutrition rates</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity uses a <cite id="23">documented theory of change</cite> focusing on the prevention and treatment of malnutrition to reduce global mortality.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking malnutrition rates ensures that feeding programs actually result in long-term health recovery.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite high efficiency, the <cite id="8">recent revenue decline</cite> suggests a need for increased donor support to maintain scale.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="4">89.7% program expense ratio</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="11">peer median of 83.0%</cite>.
It costs the charity only <cite id="12">$0.04 to raise every $1</cite> in public support, reflecting highly efficient donor acquisition.
Action Against Hunger <cite id="13">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite> and holds a <cite id="14">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Action Against Hunger's operational model is built on high-volume, low-cost delivery. Their <cite id="15">cost per beneficiary is approximately $5.46</cite>, which is considered exceptional for the extreme poverty sector. This efficiency is supported by a <cite id="16">100% independent board of directors</cite> and a <cite id="17">conflict of interest policy</cite> that ensures objective oversight. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="18">10.2 months of working capital</cite>, allowing for immediate mobilization during sudden-onset humanitarian emergencies.
rich_narrative.summary: Action Against Hunger USA is a global humanitarian leader focused on <cite id="1">eliminating life-threatening undernutrition</cite> through direct medical and nutritional interventions. Founded in <cite id="2">1979</cite>, the organization has spent over four decades developing specialized protocols to treat severe acute malnutrition in crisis zones. The charity maintains <cite id="3">exceptional financial efficiency</cite>, directing <cite id="4">89.7% of its expenses to program services</cite>. This lean operational model ensures that the vast majority of donor funds reach the field. Their work is highly targeted, focusing on the <cite id="5">prevention, detection, and treatment of undernutrition</cite>, especially in children. Beyond immediate feeding programs, they implement <cite id="6">clean water and safe sanitation</cite> initiatives to prevent the diseases that often lead to malnutrition. While the organization saw <cite id="7">total revenue of $201,188,874</cite> in 2023, it experienced a <cite id="8">revenue decline to $160,756,238</cite> in the following fiscal year. Despite this fluctuation, the charity remains a top-rated entity with a <cite id="9">95/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. Their impact is measurable and data-driven, tracking specific outcomes such as the <cite id="10">Global Acute Malnutrition Rate in Somalia</cite> to refine their response strategies.
